exercises.title: Spreadsheet Builder
exercises.description: Simulate an Excel spreadsheet using a 2D list structure. Build a `Spreadsheet` class with methods to write cells, apply formulas (SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT), and format the output as an ASCII table. Practice the concepts taught with openpyxl.
exercises.starterCode:
class Spreadsheet:
    def __init__(self, rows=20, cols=10):
        self.grid = [[""] * cols for _ in range(rows)]
        self.rows = rows
        self.cols = cols
        self._col_names = "ABCDEFGHIJ"

    def _cell_to_idx(self, cell_ref):
        """Convert 'A1' to (row=0, col=0)."""
        col_letter = cell_ref[0].upper()
        row_num = int(cell_ref[1:]) - 1
        col_num = self._col_names.index(col_letter)
        return row_num, col_num

    def write(self, cell_ref, value):
        r, c = self._cell_to_idx(cell_ref)
        self.grid[r][c] = value

    def read(self, cell_ref):
        r, c = self._cell_to_idx(cell_ref)
        return self.grid[r][c]

    def formula_sum(self, start_cell, end_cell):
        """Compute SUM of a range (same column, multiple rows)."""
        # TODO: Parse start and end cells
        # TODO: Collect all numeric values in the range
        # TODO: Return the sum
        pass

    def formula_avg(self, start_cell, end_cell):
        """Compute AVERAGE of a range."""
        # TODO: Similar to formula_sum but divide by count
        pass

    def print_range(self, start_cell, end_cell):
        """Print a range of cells as an ASCII table."""
        r1, c1 = self._cell_to_idx(start_cell)
        r2, c2 = self._cell_to_idx(end_cell)
        for r in range(r1, r2 + 1):
            row_data = [str(self.grid[r][c]) for c in range(c1, c2 + 1)]
            print(" | ".join(f"{v:>12}" for v in row_data))

# Build a sales spreadsheet
ss = Spreadsheet()

# Headers
ss.write("A1", "Month")
ss.write("B1", "Sales")
ss.write("C1", "Expenses")
ss.write("D1", "Profit")

# Data rows
months = [("Jan", 45000, 32000), ("Feb", 52000, 35000), ("Mar", 48000, 31000),
          ("Apr", 67000, 40000), ("May", 72000, 45000)]

for i, (month, sales, expenses) in enumerate(months, start=2):
    ss.write(f"A{i}", month)
    ss.write(f"B{i}", sales)
    ss.write(f"C{i}", expenses)
    ss.write(f"D{i}", sales - expenses)  # Profit formula

# Totals row
ss.write("A7", "TOTAL")
ss.write("B7", ss.formula_sum("B2", "B6"))
ss.write("C7", ss.formula_sum("C2", "C6"))
ss.write("D7", ss.formula_sum("D2", "D6"))

# Print the spreadsheet
ss.print_range("A1", "D7")
print()

# Statistics
print(f"Average monthly sales: ${ss.formula_avg('B2', 'B6'):,.0f}")
print(f"Total profit: ${ss.read('D7'):,}")
hints: formula_sum: parse both cells with _cell_to_idx, collect self.grid[r][c] for r in range(r1, r2+1)
Filter numeric: values = [v for v in cells if isinstance(v, (int, float))]
formula_avg: return sum(values) / len(values) if values else 0
print_range format: f'{v:>12}' right-aligns values in 12-char columns.

exercises.title: CSV Report Generator
exercises.description: Simulate generating an Excel-style report as a formatted text output. Given sales data, generate a multi-section report with: a data table, summary statistics, and a top-performers section. This practices the data manipulation concepts behind openpyxl reporting.
exercises.starterCode:
from collections import defaultdict

SALES_DATA = [
    {"rep": "Alice",  "region": "North", "product": "Widget", "q1": 12000, "q2": 15000, "q3": 14000, "q4": 18000},
    {"rep": "Bob",    "region": "South", "product": "Gadget", "q1": 8000,  "q2": 9500,  "q3": 11000, "q4": 13000},
    {"rep": "Carol",  "region": "East",  "product": "Widget", "q1": 10000, "q2": 12000, "q3": 15000, "q4": 17000},
    {"rep": "Dave",   "region": "West",  "product": "Tool",   "q1": 6000,  "q2": 7000,  "q3": 8000,  "q4": 9500},
    {"rep": "Eve",    "region": "North", "product": "Gadget", "q1": 14000, "q2": 16000, "q3": 18000, "q4": 22000},
]

def generate_report(data):
    """Generate a full sales report."""
    # Add annual totals
    for row in data:
        row["annual"] = row["q1"] + row["q2"] + row["q3"] + row["q4"]

    # Section 1: Data table
    print("ANNUAL SALES REPORT")
    print("=" * 70)
    print(f"{'Rep':<10} {'Region':<10} {'Q1':>8} {'Q2':>8} {'Q3':>8} {'Q4':>8} {'Annual':>10}")
    print("-" * 70)
    for row in sorted(data, key=lambda r: -r["annual"]):
        print(f"{row['rep']:<10} {row['region']:<10} "
              f"${row['q1']:>7,} ${row['q2']:>7,} ${row['q3']:>7,} ${row['q4']:>7,} ${row['annual']:>9,}")

    # Section 2: Summary
    print()
    print("SUMMARY")
    print("-" * 40)
    total = sum(r["annual"] for r in data)
    # TODO: Print total annual revenue, average per rep, top performer, best region

    # Section 3: Regional breakdown
    print()
    print("BY REGION")
    print("-" * 40)
    # TODO: Group by region, sum annual sales, print sorted by revenue desc

generate_report(SALES_DATA)
hints: For regional totals: regions = defaultdict(int); for r in data: regions[r['region']] += r['annual']
Sort regions for display: sorted(regions.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Column letter to index
warmup.starterCode:
col_names = "ABCDEFGHIJ"
for cell in ["A1", "C3", "J2"]:
    col = cell[0]
    row = int(cell[1:]) - 1  # 0-based row
    col_idx = col_names.index(col)  # 0-based col
    print(f"{cell} -> row={row}, col={col_idx}")
warmup.expectedOutput:
A1 -> row=0, col=0
C3 -> row=2, col=2
J2 -> row=1, col=9
